description:
Goal: make ABI and emission readiness a Compiler2-owned artifact projection before backend adapters consume it. Signal: the artifact ladder is explicit and tested: MaterializedProgram(root) -> AbiReadyProgram(root) -> EmissionReadyProgram(root). Backends consume the final closed facts mechanically and never rediscover reachability, dispatch, return delivery, type flow, callable entries, or extern marshal classes.

Strategy: split durable compiler facts from old-world backend implementation artifacts, then add the minimal Compiler2 facts in dependency order. We keep the current fz-rh2.8.2 adapter ticket blocked until this model is documented and backed by tests, so when this epic closes we return to fz-rh2.8.2 with a smaller, tractable problem.

Design constraints:
- No shims, bridges, adapters, or fallback paths to old planner/codegen discovery.
- Do not copy old SpecPlan, SpecRegistry, AbiFacts, native_fns, cont_fns, cont_target_fns, cont_extras_count, or callable wrapper signatures into Compiler2 facts.
- Keep old-world lessons only where they express durable invariants: call edges, return delivery, extern marshal classes, effects, callable-boundary obligations, and stable emission inventory.
- Telemetry emits raw ids/facts/structures only; handlers render or summarize.

Checks:
- Quicksort + uncalled foo proves the artifact frontier remains closed and cold functions stay cold.
- Enum.reduce proves callable-entry obligations are explicit and sufficient.
- Variadic extern fixtures prove marshal classes are frozen at the artifact boundary.
- Tests fail if backend-facing work asks semantic/type/reachability questions after MaterializedProgram.
- Docs describe the final model before fz-rh2.8.2 resumes.
title: Compiler2 artifacts: settle ABI and emission-ready fact model
